title: More than 1,100 Dead Olive Ridley Turtles Washed Ashore
what_talking_about: Over 1,100 dead olive ridley turtles appeared on Tamil Nadu beaches during the species' nesting season (September through March).
Large trawler nets entangle turtles, preventing them from surfacing for air and causing suffocation.
Tamil Nadu's 9 km offshore trawler ban lacks enforcement, and turtle excluder devices remain rarely installed.
why_care: These endangered turtles maintain marine ecosystems by feeding on seabed invertebrates and crustaceans, releasing locked oxygen.
The incident underscores urgent needs for stricter fishing regulations, industry awareness, and coordinated conservation efforts.

sources.title: Mongabay: Over 1,100 dead olive ridley turtles wash ashore in southern India

subtitle: It's a wake-up call for  stricter fishing regulations
